channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Strands #816 Hints and Answers for Today, May 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #816 for Thursday, May 28, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, May 28, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #816 are all terms related to fragrances.

General Strands Hints For May 28

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Elon.
  • Hint 2: The princess in Aladdin.
  • Hint 3: French Butter.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #816 for May 28, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for May 27, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • FORT
  • MINK
  • PEAS
  • GRAN
  • SUGAR

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #816 On May 28, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • SANDALWOOD
  • PEAR
  • BERGAMOT
  • NEROLI
  • JASMIN
  • MUSK
  • SPANAGRAM: FRAGRANCE

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #816?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on May 29 will be Strands Game #817.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-816-hints-and-answers-for-today-may-28-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Destruction AllStars Pulled from PlayStation Store
item.content:encoded:
Another PlayStation exclusive has been pulled from the PlayStation Store. An email recently confirmed that Destruction AllStars, a 2021 release, has been pulled from the online store. Additionally, the game’s live-service features will remain offline, including online multiplayer.

Developed by Lucid Games and published by Sony, this was a vehicle combat game that was exclusive to PlayStation platforms. The game was originally planned to be a launch title for PS5 in November 2020, but was pushed back to April 2021.

Destruction AllStars Single-Player Mode Stays Until November

The only part of the game that is sticking around, at least until November, is the single-player mode. He confirmed that the single-player mode will stick around until November 25. Beyond that, solo arcade modes will stick around but with limited functionality.

“Due to ongoing technical issues, multiplayer services for Destruction AllStars on PlayStation 5 consoles shall remain offline and are no longer available,” reads the statement (via Press Start).

Unfortunately, many live service games have been shut down this year. Highguard lasted less than 2 months before being shut down. Amazon Games’ New World: Aeternum is slated to be taken offline next year. Concord, another Sony exclusive, was shut down after a very short life cycle in 2024. The game reportedly cost over $400 million to make. Learn more about that here.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates if new information becomes available.

How do you feel about this shutdown of yet another PlayStation exclusive? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/destruction-allstars-pulled-from-playstation-store/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Remedy CEO Says AI Can’t Replicate Games Like Alan Wake 2
item.content:encoded:
The new Remedy CEO offers comments on AI and his specific viewpoint on why it won’t make game development cheaper anytime soon.

Jean-Charles Gaudechon, a former EA executive, was recently announced as the new CEO of Remedy. Taking on the role at the head of the CONTROL developer, he’s now talking about one of the most discussed topics in all of gaming, the use of AI and how it will power the future.

Remedy CEO Says AI Won’t Make Game Development Cheaper Soon

During an interview with Christopher Dring of The Game Business, Jean-Charles Gaudechon was asked whether he believes automated technology will help game development. He emphasized the importance of human creativity in Remedy’s releases and explained why he doesn’t believe AI will make game development cheaper for the foreseeable future.

“My stance is that AI is not going to make things cheaper, and it’s not going to make things cheaper for a pretty long time. People thinking, ‘Oh, we can now make games cheaper with this’, I have big question marks around that. And what types of games by the way? I said this during the earnings call, good luck trying to do Alan Wake 2 with AI. Try to use Genie and do that, and we’ll see where you land.

“But we’ve always been an R&D studio. It’s always dabbling with tech stuff. So, I’m not going to block people from doing so. But we’re going to be extremely cautious on that, never getting anywhere close to creative and anywhere close to user-facing.”

AI has been widely discussed in gaming recently. PlayStation went so far as to say that they believe AI will be powering the future of their games. They are also working closely with Bandai Namco on generative AI technology. Learn more about that here.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/remedy-ceo-says-ai-cant-replicate-games-like-alan-wake-2/

item.title: Donkey Kong 64 Finally Joins Nintendo Switch Online
item.content:encoded:
Donkey Kong 64 is now available on Nintendo Switch Online as part of the Nintendo 64 classics. Nintendo announced that the game will join the service on June 4, just in time to celebrate the one-year anniversary of the Switch 2’s release.

Until the release of Donkey Kong Bananza in 2025, Donkey Kong 64 was the only 3D adventure featuring Donkey Kong. While the wild character is set to return to the three-dimensional world, fan-favorites like Lanky Kong and Chunky Kong have yet to make a comeback in any games.

What’s Next for Nintendo?

With the month of June upon us and several gaming showcases announced, everyone is still waiting for the announcement of the first general Nintendo Direct presentation of the year. It is known that Star Fox is returning in a remake of Star Fox 64 on the Nintendo 64 with enhanced visuals and more. Much like Donkey Kong, Fox McCloud is seeing a resurgence spurred by an appearance in a Super Mario Bros movie.

As for other potential announcements from Nintendo, everyone is still expecting the announcement of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake. Reliable, weaker NateTheHate previously claimed that the game would be released later this year. The next Fire Emblem installment still needs a release date, and players are hoping for an announcement on either the next 3D Mario experience or a Super Smash Bros. title. For more on what could be coming at a Nintendo Direct, click here.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

Former Rockstar Developers Haven’t Matched GTA’s Success, Says CEO

Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick comments on the gap between GTA releases and discusses why other developers haven’t been able to match Rockstar Games’ success.

Mina the Hollower is a New Indie GOTY Contender

Looking at the early ratings, Mina the Hollower has proven itself as a contender for the indie GOTY award in 2026.

The Witcher 3 Songs of the Past DLC Brings Updated PC System Requirements

The Witcher 3 will get a new set of minimum PC system requirements due to the Songs of the Past expansion.

No Man’s Sky The Swarm Update Details Revealed

No Man’s Sky players will be facing off against The Swarm in the month of May thanks to a new update. Here are more details.

item.title: Pokémon Game Sales Surpass 515 Million Worldwide
item.content:encoded:
The Pokémon Company confirms Pokémon game sales have surpassed 515 million units, including 26 million sold last year. In its latest financial earnings report, The Pokémon Company revealed that by the end of the fiscal year ending in 2016, it had sold up to 515 million video games.

At the end of March 2025, that number was 489 million. This means that 26 million Pokémon games were sold in the last fiscal year. It’s known that at least 4 million of those sales are from Pokémon Pokopia.

Pokémon Remains One of Gaming’s Best-Selling Franchises Ever

In the financial earnings report, it was noted that over 85 billion Pokémon trading cards have been produced for the Pokémon franchise. Regarding the consistency of the video games, the Pokémon Company stated that the core Pokémon experience has remained unchanged and continues to engage new generations of players.

“Ever since the launch of the “Pokémon Red” and “Pokémon Green” versions in 1996, the basic aspects of “Pokémon” video games remain unchanged: players collect Pokémon, engage them in battles, raise them to progressively higher levels, and trade them with others.”

The next mainline Pokémon games are scheduled to release in 2027. Pokémon Winds and Waves is out next year. However, there’s no release date at this time. While there is no release date, there is a potential price leak from Amazon. Learn more about that at this link.

Speaking of Pokopia, the European region will soon begin selling Nintendo Switch 2 bundles that include the game. Learn more about that here.

What do you want to see for the future of Pokémon? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former Rockstar Developers Haven’t Matched GTA’s Success, Says CEO
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick comments on the gap between GTA releases and discusses why other developers haven’t been able to match Rockstar Games’ success.

Rockstar Games plans on finally releasing Grand Theft Auto 6 in November of this year, 13 years after the release of GTA 5. It’s not as though GTA 5 has struggled in any way, however. The game continues to sell 5 million games per quarter, and GTA Online continues to get quality-of-life updates that have secured its longevity as well. Now, Zelnick is discussing what “scares” Take-Two and what he believes will ultimately lead to another property rising up to truly combat GTA.

Take-Two CEO Says Creativity, Not Tech, Will Challenge GTA

During the TD Cowen 54th Annual Technology, Media & Telecom conference call, Zelnick Press stated that many have tried to create a massive hit the size of GTA or any of the major properties in Take-Two’s portfolio; however, they haven’t found success. He even alluded to former Rockstar employees unsuccessfully trying to match that success.

“Lots of other people have tried. Lots and lots, including former Rockstar employees. So far, they haven’t been able to do it. Doesn’t mean they can’t in the future, by the way. We’re always running scared. But it won’t be technology that changes the game. That won’t be the change. What’ll change is that some extraordinarily creative individual or individuals will show up and do something astonishing. Our goal is to get those people to work within the Take-Two system. If we fail to do that, we fail,” he said.

MindsEye is a single-player cinematic action game developed by Build A Rocket Boy, a studio founded by former Rockstar North President Leslie Benzies.

For anyone worried, Zelnick has confirmed the release date of GTA 6 at least three times in the last month. Learn more here.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/former-rockstar-developers-havent-matched-gtas-success-says-ceo/

item.title: Mina the Hollower is a New Indie GOTY Contender
item.content:encoded:
It seems like there’s a new indie Game of the Year contender in the race, and it’s none other than Yacht Club Games’ Mina the Hollower.

Mina the Hollower, the highly anticipated 2D action platformer from the hands of Yacht Club Games, released earlier this month, and it has been faring quite well across critics and players as well. Actually, it has been doing so well that it seems like the title could take home the Game of the Year award in the indie category.

Mina The Hollower is Currently the Highest Rated Game of the Year

The reviews are out, and Mina the Hollower has been progressing with flying colors. At the moment, if you head over to the Metacritic site, you will observe that Yacht Club Games’ latest platformer is now the highest-rated game on the website. It’s doing better than some of the biggest AAA games that have been released this year, including Forza Horizon 6, Pokémon Pokopia, Resident Evil Requiem, 007 First Light, and more.

Mina the Hollower comes from the hands of the people who brought us another gem back in the day, Shovel Knight. The title brings players back to the Game Boy era with 8-bit visuals, offering varied platforming traversal mechanics and the ability to beat up monsters with your whip or an arsenal of weapons. The goal for Mina, the inventor, is to restore the Spark Generators to save Tenebrous Isle from a curse while burrowing through a variety of exotic locales.

In other news, the Shovel Knight developers revealed that Mina the Hollower’s performance will determine their fate. Additionally, Hounded, one of the participants at the Insider Gaming Showcase, has announced a brand-new publisher deal to release the game. Do you think Mina the Hollower will win indie GOTY? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt’s updated PC system requirements. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: The Witcher 3 Songs of the Past DLC Brings Updated PC System Requirements
item.content:encoded:
CD Projekt Red reveals that Songs of the Past DLC will update The Witcher 3’s PC system requirements.

After months of anticipation and various leaks and rumors, the third DLC for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt has finally been announced. The expansion, titled “Songs of the Past,” is scheduled for release in 2027. Along with this news, players have been informed that there have been changes to the game’s system requirements for running it on PC.

The Witcher 3 New PC System Requirements, No More Windows 10 and HDD Support

A new post on X (formerly Twitter) reveals that players will have to abide by a new set of PC system requirements for The Witcher 3 once the Songs of the Past Expansion drops. The developers have stated that the updated requirements will “ensure smooth performance and compatibility going forward.” Check out the new list of minimum requirements below:

  • CPU: AMD Ryzen 5 2600, Intel Core i5-8400
  • GPU: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660, AMD Radeon RX 5500 XT 8GB
  • VRAM: 6 GB
  • RAM: 12 GB
  • Storage: 70 GB SSD
  • OS: 64-bit Windows 11

The support page states that Windows 11 will be the minimum requirement for both The Witcher 3 and Cyberpunk 2077. Moreover, no more HDD support, only SSD will be supported for faster load times, smoother asset streaming, and improved performance. Lastly, the game will only run on DirectX 12 and on processors and graphics cards supported on Windows 11. However, it is possible to revert to an earlier version.

In other news, Alexander Morton, the voice actor for Zoltan in The Witcher series, has passed away. Additionally, the Witcher 4 team has expanded to 499 developers at CD Projekt Red, showing the studio’s commitment to its next big RPG. What are your thoughts on the updated PC system requirements for The Witcher 3? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about No Man’s Sky’s The Swarm update. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: LoL Dev Update: Locke, Ranked 5s, and Mayhem Traits Removed
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has released League of Legends 2026 Season 2 Dev Update with some pretty big announcements for the MOBA.

The publishers have previewed the next champion in the game and announced the return of Ranked 5s for full premade teams.

Plus there are major changes to ARAM Mayhem, including the removal of Traits and a new ARAM Mayhem purchasable.

Here are all of the announcements.

Locke

Riot has officially confirmed that LoL’s next champion will be Locke, an AP assassin.

While not mentioned in the dev update, it has been confirmed that he will be the only new LoL champion released in 2026 as Riot focuses on VGUs.

Riot hasn’t released any other information, though alleged Locke gameplay screenshots leaked earlier this year.

ARAM Mayhem

Riot has also announced a lot of changes to ARAM Mayhem, including the removal of the Traits system in Patch 26.12.

“Rather than provide increased strategy, it often felt like you lost options because it was trolling not to choose a traited augment.

“And beyond this illusion of choice, it felt gigabad in games where you just get unlucky and can’t level up any of your traits,” Ezra ‘Riot Phlox’ Lynn explained.

However, Riot has acknowledged that some traits were still popular and will return to the game mode in the form of new augments.

In fact, Riot has promised to add around 50 new augments to ARAM Mayhem, including one that turns snowball into a Sharknado.

And there will also be a new type of augment: Ability Augments.

These will enhance a player’s champion’s abilities. For example, Multishot will split a projectile into three projectiles in different directions, allowing for three Blitzcrank hooks at once.

Riot also wants to focus on Quest Augments, with a new one called Quest: Tooth Fairy, which means that dealing a chunk of damage within a short window to an enemy champion will cause them to drop a tooth which grants Lethality and Magic Pen for every tooth you’ve extracted.

Additionally, Riot is testing a new purchasable on the Brazilian and Oceania servers called the ARAM Wardrobe.

This will cost 1350 RP and temporarily give players access to most Epic and below skins, including legacy, released before 2026 in both ARAM and ARAM Mayhem.

Ranked 5s Return to LoL

Riot has also announced the return of Ranked fives, allowing players to play ranked in pre-made five-player parties, no matter their friends’ ranks.

The mode will use the same pick and bans system as pro play: three bans > three picks > two bans > two picks.

However, this will now be an experimental queue that isn’t available permanently. Instead, it will be playable on weekends at set times, between June 26 to Setepmber 6.

Riot found that having Ranked 5s as a permanent mode led to a lot of games where the teams weren’t balanced because of the number of players queuing up.

The publisher believes keeping it to a temporary release will lead to better queue times and fairer matchmaking.

What do you make of Riot’s announcements? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Additionally, Riot has also released LoL Patch 26.11 with big updates for tank supports and ranged toplaners.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lol-dev-update-locke-ranked-5s-and-mayhem-traits-removed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: No Man’s Sky The Swarm Update Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Hello Games has just introduced a brand-new update for No Man’s Sky, The Swarm, which launches today.

No Man’s Sky received a big update just last month with Xeno Arena, which basically added Pokémon in space for the game. Now, the developers have stated that it was a small one, and the next update “is something much larger”. Learn more about what The Swarm brings to No Man’s Sky below:

No Man’s Sky Brings Something Huge in the Sky Above the Planets

The new blog post reveals that the Swarm update for No Man’s Sky will require a community effort. Players participating in this update will undergo a personality test that places them into one of three factions. These factions need to work together to overpower the Hive of Glass, which is threatening the existence of the universe.

Players will launch a war effort from the Nexus and receive a new bulletin to track their progress. When facing off with the Hive, your progress will be seen by all players, and the squad that provides the best effort will be “memorialized in the Space Anomaly for all time.”

No Man’s Sky’s The Swarm update will bring the most epic space battles to date, battling against a swarm of robotic drones, with hundreds of ships participating. The big thing in the sky, or the Hive, is a weapon in itself, the largest one players have seen in No Man’s Sky, which is “capable of destroying freighter fleets and perhaps even something as large as a space station” through its laser.

You will be battling in space and investigating the ruins of the drones on the planets through the Gravity Gun. The investigations will lead to the discovery not only of the Hive’s origins but also of how to sabotage it and the weaknesses players can exploit.

Your efforts will not be in vain, as the developers have set up rewards such as a full suit of armor with a retro sci-fi feel, a new rifle, a huge new jetpack, and much more.

In other news, read about No Man’s Sky founder saying “jokes on them” in response to Once Human allegedly stealing assets. What are your thoughts on No Man’s Sky’s The Hive Update? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Steam Deck prices rising as much as $300. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/no-mans-sky-the-swarm-update-details-revealed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: League of Legends Patch 26.11 Nerfs and Buffs
item.content:encoded:
League of Legends (LoL) Patch 26.11 has had its patch notes released by Riot Games with some major changes.

Riot has taken substantial steps to return tank supports to the League of Legends meta.

And there are big adjustments for toplaners too, with a Heartsteel buff and a nerf to Hexplate for ranged users.

Here are all the major changes from the large LoL Patch 26.11.

Support Changes

Ranged supports have dominated the 2026 meta, especially in pro play.

And one major reason for that is that the new role quests stop supports from roaming as much.

Riot is helping melee supports, especially ones that like to engage, with big item changes and improvements to Grubs to make roaming for them more worthwhile.

Grubs

  • Damage Per Tick, Per Grub: 3 / 9 / 12 || 1.5 / 4.5 / 6 ⇒ 4 / 12 / 16 || 2 / 6 / 8
  • Summoned Voidmite HP: 60% of Melee Minion ⇒ 100% of Melee Minion

Summon Aery

Summon Aery’s early game defensive output is being brought down so lane trades can stick onto enchanter duos better.

  • Shielding: 30 – 100 (Levels 1 – 18) (+10% bonus AD) (+5% AP) ⇒ 20 – 100 (Levels 1 – 18) (+10% bonus AD) (+5% AP)

Aftershock

Riot is buffing Aftershock in the early game so aggressive users can better trade with their opponents.

  • Armor and MR: 35 (+80% bonus) ⇒ 45 (+75% bonus)

Guardian

And finally, we are buffing Guardian’s cooldown and adjusting how it scales to help tankier users who need it to survive the laning phase.

  • Cooldown: 90 – 40 (Level 1 – 18) ⇒ 75 – 40 (Level 1 – 18)
  • Shield: 45 – 180 (+25% AP) (+5% bonus HP) ⇒ 40 – 150 (+20% AP) (+6% bonus HP)

Dream Maker

Dream Maker is getting a mix of adjustments to function better, with a buff in some ways and nerfs in others.

Blue Bubble Damage Reduction is being adjusted from only affecting the literal first instance of damage (i.e. the first tick of a DoT) to affecting the first cast source (i.e. the entire DoT, until it runs out of flat damage reduction.)

Meanwhile, Purple Bubble Damage is changing from mostly an on-hit effect to an on-hit or on-ability damage cast source effect with AoE penalties. (i.e. If Ezreal Ult triggers the Purple Bubble, it will apply to that entire Ezreal Ult but with reduced AoE efficacy.)

  • Blue Bubble Damage Reduction: 75 – 255 (Level 1 – 18) ⇒ 50 – 194 (Level 1 – 18)
  • Blue Bubble Damage Reduction: Damage reduction affects the next non-0 instance of damage, regardless of how large it is ⇒ Damage reduction affects the next non-0 damage cast source (temporal carry-over)
  • Purple Bubble Damage: 50 – 170 (Level 1 – 18) ⇒ 40 – 160 (Level 1 – 18)
  • Purple Bubble Damage: Damage triggers on hit, or on certain abilities / items ⇒ Damage triggers on next damaging attack or ability, reduced for AoE effects (i.e. Nami E)

Moonstone Renewer

Moonstone will no longer benefits twice from heal and shield power, which is mostly a nerf but will occasionally help when opponents have Grievous Wounds / Shield Reaver.

  • Fixed various bugs, including no longer double-dipping on both heal / shield power and Grievous Wounds / Shield Reaver
  • Bounces now prioritize lower health allies

Imperial Mandate

Imperial Mandate is getting reworked to become a better fit for utility casters rather than just Nami.

  • Build Path: Fiendish Codex (850g) + Bandleglass Mirror (900g) + 500g = 2250g ⇒ Blasting Wand (850g) + Bandleglass Mirror (900g) + 650g = 2400g
  • Ability Power: 60 ⇒ 65
  • Ability Haste: 20 ⇒ 15
  • Mana Regen: 125% ⇒ 150%
  • REMOVED Unique – Coordinated Fire
  • NEW Unique – Control: Gain 15 Ability Haste for your abilities with Immobilizing effects
  • NEW Unique – Command: On immobilizing an enemy champion, mark them as 6% Vulnerable for 4s (Vulnerable enemies take % increased damage)

Echoes of Helia

A straightforward nerf for enchanters.

  • Damage Stored: 35% ⇒ 30%

Locket of the Iron Solari

And a straightforward buff for tanks.

  • Armor and Magic Resist: 25 ⇒ 30

Knight’s Vow

For Knight’s Vow, Riot is pushing its unique effect rather than generically buffing its stats or gold cost.

  • Damage Redirection: 12% ⇒ 14%
  • Heal: 10% ⇒ 12%

Zeke’s Convergence

Zeke’s is getting a quality of life improvement for any users who might be casting their ultimate from long range such as Leona or Nautilus.

  • Frostfire Tempest: Triggers on Ult cast ⇒ Readies on Ult cast for the next 5 seconds, triggering the moment an enemy champion gets within the range or after 5 seconds

LoL Patch 26.11 Champion Buffs

Diana

After previous changes to Dusk and Dawn, Diana jungle has been underwhelming.

  • Base Stats
    • Monster Damage: 230% ⇒ 270%
    • W – Pale Cascade
    • Bonus Health: 9% ⇒ 11%

Ekko

Now that the dust has settled from 26.9’s Dusk and Dawn changes, Riot says Ekko has some room for a compensation buff now that he’s not bound to that item.

  • Passive – Z-Drive Resonance
    • Per-Enemy Cooldown: 5 seconds ⇒ 4 seconds

Heimerdinger

After ranged minion aggro pull was removed last patch, how Heimerdinger’s turrets interact with waves has become a bit awkward.

It led to turrets tanking minion autos without attacking back.

Additionally, his Evolution Turrets sometimes fail to follow up on his Storm Grenade when they lose vision of the target too quickly due to minor timing fluctuations around vision and turret logic, so Riot is increasing the vision duration slightly to resolve that.

  • Q – H-28G Evolution Turret
    • Turret Attack Range: 530 ⇒ 550
    • Turret Vision Range: 585 ⇒ 650
    • NEW Turret Range: Turrets now gain 50 additional range against minions if nothing else is in range
  • E – CH-2 Electron Storm Grenade
    • Vision of target location: 1 second ⇒ 1.25 seconds

Kassadin

Our Void Walker has been limping around the Rift more than blinking. Stackable tear items have provided an additional cool late-game fantasy option besides pure AP, but his early game has been tougher than usual (in part due to our mid quest adjustment). These changes should help his laning, both in farming and trading.

  • Q – Null Sphere
    • Cooldown: 10 / 9.5 / 9 / 8.5 / 8 ⇒ 9 / 8.5 / 8 / 7.5 / 7
  • W – Nether Blade
    • Base Magic Damage: 20 (+10% AP) ⇒ 25 (+10% AP)

Quinn

Riot is continuing its efforts to make Quinn work as a jungler by helping her jungle clear.

  • Passive – Harrier
    • Monster Damage: 50 ⇒ 75
  • Q – Blinding Assault
    • Monster Damage: 150% ⇒ 200%

LoL Patch 26.11 Champion Nerfs

Brand

Brand has been toeing the line in bot for a while now, and this patch has well and truly crossed over, so we’re targeting that lane with a nerf to his base armor.

  • Base Stats
    • Base Armor: 27 ⇒ 24

Teemo

Riot is nerfing Teemo’s Statikk Shiv build so that it’s still in a good place but that his AP build paths are also viable.

  • Base Stats
    • Armor Growth: 4.95 per level ⇒ 4.5 per level
  • E – Toxic Shot
    • On-hit/Poison per tick scaling: (+10% bonus AD/+20% bonus AD) ⇒ (+5% bonus AD/+10% bonus AD)

LoL Patch 26.11 Champion Adjustments

Smolder

After the Smolder hotfix added to the last patch, the baby dragon is getting even more changes.

These latest nerfs are pushing towards him returning to crit-focused builds rather than Essence Reaver into bruiser.

  • Passive – Dragon Practice
    • Bonus Magic Damage on Q: 40% * (1 + (Crit Chance *.5 * (1 + Bonus Crit Damage)) of Passive stacks (60% at 100% Crit, 66% if you have IE) ⇒ 25% * (1 + (Crit Chance * 1.2 * (1 + Bonus Crit Damage)) of Passive stacks
    • Bonus Magic Damage on E: 12% of Passive stacks ⇒ 8% * (1 + (Crit Chance * .6 * (1 + Bonus Crit Damage)) of Passive stacks

Xin Zhao

Riot is nerfing AP Xin Zhao while compensating by helping his sustained damage.

  • Passive – Determination
    • Bonus Damage: 15 / 30 / 45 / 60% Bonus AD (Levels 1 / 6 / 11 / 16) ⇒ 15 / 30 / 45 / 60% Bonus AD (+5 / 10 / 15 / 20% AP) (Levels 1 / 6 / 11 / 16)
    • Heal Amount: 3 / 4 / 5% Max HP (+50 / 65 / 90% AP) (Levels 1 / 6 / 11) ⇒ 3 / 4 / 5% Max HP (+45 / 55 / 80% AP) (Levels 1 / 6 / 11)

Item Changes

Experimental Hexplate

Riot is addressing Hexplate on ranged toplaners, with Vayne and Varus top becoming far too oppressive, but wants it to remain a viable option for a lot of different archetypes.

  • Overdrive: On ulting, 50% bonus AS and 20% bonus MS for 8 seconds ⇒ Ranged: 35% bonus AS and 14% bonus MS for 8 seconds (unchanged for melee)

Heartsteel

Riot claims that with the Mundo meta out of the way, it can now revert a Heartstell nerf from a year ago since Rioters feel the item occupies a really cool (and important) aspect of greedy tank itemization.

  • Damage To Permanent Health Conversion Rate: 8% ⇒ 10%

Statikk Shiv

Riot is buffing Shiv for AD champions that could really benefit from it but claims to be keeping a close eye on how the item performs.

  • Attack Damage: 40 ⇒ 45

Are you happy with tank supports coming back? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

There could be even bigger changes coming to the bot lane as the League Next update will overhaul the support role.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
